skill.name: doc-generator
skill.description: Generate Markdown documentation from Python codebases by analyzing files, docstrings, type hints, classes, functions, and module structure.
skill.summary: Create structured Markdown documentation for Python modules, APIs, and README files.
skill.author: bossjones

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown fences, inline Markdown examples, and documentation text. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempts, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences or inline examples in a documentation template. They do not invoke a shell, execute Ruby, or instruct the assistant to run external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows Markdown output examples and names of Python standard library modules. No shell command syntax or executable code path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Text Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography matches occur inside the skill description and Markdown documentation examples. No hashing, encryption, checksum generation, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains prose about docstrings, parameters, return types, and missing descriptions. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic logic.

content.value_statement: Python projects often lack clear module and API documentation. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code turn Python structure into readable Markdown.
actual_capabilities: Generates Markdown documentation for Python files or directories.
Extracts module, class, function, and method information from Python code.
Uses docstrings and type hints to create readable API references.
Organizes documentation by modules, classes, methods, and functions.
Adds table of contents guidance for larger modules.
Handles missing docstrings and syntax errors with warnings.
limitations: The skill describes an analysis workflow but does not include executable tooling.
Generated usage examples may need review for project-specific behavior.
Runtime introspection may be unsafe for untrusted Python modules.
Private members are skipped unless the user asks to include them.

use_cases.title: Create API References
use_cases.description: Generate structured Markdown pages from Python modules, classes, methods, and functions.

use_cases.title: Refresh Project READMEs
use_cases.description: Build README sections that summarize public APIs, examples, parameters, and return values.

use_cases.title: Document Internal Services
use_cases.description: Turn service code into readable module documentation for onboarding and review.

prompt_templates.title: Document One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single module.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Markdown documentation for this Python file. Include module summary, classes, functions, parameters, return values, and missing docstring notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a README Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Python package and draft README documentation. Include the public API, short usage examples, and setup notes if they are visible.

prompt_templates.title: Build an API Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an API reference for this Python directory. Organize output by module, class, method, and function. Preserve useful docstring formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Documentation Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Python codebase for documentation coverage. Generate Markdown docs and list missing docstrings, weak examples, unclear parameters, and incomplete return descriptions.

output_examples.input: A Python module with one documented class, two methods, and one standalone helper function.
output_examples.output: A Markdown API page with a module summary, class section, method descriptions, parameter notes, return value descriptions, and a helper function section.

output_examples.input: A package directory with several modules and mixed docstring coverage.
output: A package overview with a table of contents.
Separate module sections for public classes and functions.
Warnings for missing docstrings and files with syntax errors.

output_examples.input: A README update request for a small Python utility library.
output_examples.output: A README-ready documentation section with purpose, public API summary, basic usage guidance, and notes about missing type hints.

best_practices: Review generated examples before publishing them as official usage guidance.
Prefer AST parsing for untrusted code instead of runtime introspection.
Run the skill on focused modules first, then expand to full directories.
anti_patterns: Do not execute untrusted Python modules only to collect documentation.
Do not publish generated docs without checking public and private API boundaries.
Do not treat missing docstrings as complete documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill execute project code?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends AST parsing and only mentions inspection as an option. Avoid runtime introspection for untrusted modules.

faq.question: What languages does it document?
faq.answer: It is designed for Python codebases and Python documentation patterns.

faq.question: Can it create README files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft README sections from visible Python structure, docstrings, type hints, and examples.

faq.question: Does it require external dependencies?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends standard Python modules such as ast, inspect, and pathlib.

faq.question: How does it handle missing docstrings?
faq.answer: It notes missing descriptions and continues generating documentation from available structure and type hints.

faq.question: Can it include private members?
faq.answer: Yes, but only when requested. The default guidance skips members with leading underscores.
